title: antirrhinum majus subsp. charidemi
intro: Antirrhinum majus subsp. charidemi, also known as the snapdragon or charidemi, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its health benefits, including improved immune function and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of Antirrhinum majus subsp. charidemi can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and teas, and are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
